questions.explanation: A CRS specifies how coordinates map to real-world locations, typically combining a datum (Earth model) and a projection (map transformation).

options.A: How spatial data is symbolized on a map
options.B: How coordinates relate to the Earth using a datum and a projection
options.C: The file format used to store vector features
options.D: The database schema for spatial tables

questions.question: In GIS, what does a coordinate reference system (CRS) define?

questions.explanation: WHERE filters rows before aggregation and ordering are applied.
questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows returned by a SELECT query?

questions.explanation: git checkout -b <name> creates a branch and checks it out in one step.
questions.question: Which Git command creates a new branch named feature/geocode and switches to it?

questions.explanation: GeoPackage (.gpkg) is an OGC standard built on SQLite that can store multiple layers in one file.
questions.question: Which format is a single-file SQLite-based container that can store multiple vector layers and attributes?
questions.topic: Geospatial Data Formats & Standards (Geojson, Shapefile, Geopackage, Ogc)

questions.explanation: ST_Intersection returns the actual intersecting geometry; ST_Intersects only returns a boolean.
questions.question: In PostGIS, which function returns the geometry representing the intersection of two geometries?

questions.explanation: ArcGIS Server is the service engine that publishes and serves GIS web services; Portal handles identity/content, Data Store stores hosted data.
questions.question: In ArcGIS Enterprise, which component primarily hosts and serves web services (map/feature services) to clients?
questions.topic: Esri Arcgis Platform (Arcgis Pro, Arcgis Enterprise, Arcgis Online)

questions.explanation: L.geoJSON is designed to ingest GeoJSON and create corresponding Leaflet layers.
questions.question: In Leaflet, which object type is most appropriate for displaying a GeoJSON FeatureCollection on a map?

questions.explanation: GDAL provides server-friendly CLI tools that can be scripted for batch raster reprojection, clipping, and conversion at scale.

options.A: Use ArcGIS Pro interactively and export results manually
options.B: Use GDAL tools (e.g., gdalwarp, gdal_translate) scripted in Python
options.C: Use GeoJSON as an intermediate format for rasters
options.D: Use Git to store rasters and rely on diffs for processing

questions.question: You are building a geoprocessing pipeline that must reproject, clip, and convert thousands of rasters nightly. You need robust command-line automation and good performance on servers. Which approach is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: OGC API - Features is the modern OGC standard for querying vector features over HTTP with filtering and paging.

options.A: ArcGIS REST Feature Service query endpoint

questions.question: A client requests an OGC-compliant way to query features with filtering and paging over HTTP, and they want to avoid vendor-specific endpoints. Which API best fits?

questions.explanation: Geological mapping benefits from topology validation and snapping to structural features (faults/contacts) to reduce gaps/overlaps and slivers after overlays.

options.A: Digitize polygons without topology rules to preserve raw field interpretation
options.B: Apply topology rules (e.g., Must Not Overlap, Must Not Have Gaps) and snap/validate edits against fault lines before final overlay
options.C: Convert all polygons to raster to eliminate topology issues permanently
options.D: Store the map as GeoJSON only, because GeoJSON enforces topology

questions.question: You are compiling a geological map and need to ensure polygon contacts align with mapped faults and avoid sliver polygons after overlay operations. Which workflow best addresses this?
